2_Learning_Roadmaps: Backend Developer Path: JavaScript/TypeScript → Node.js → Go or Java
Frontend Developer Path: HTML/CSS → JavaScript → TypeScript → React
Full-stack Path: JavaScript/TypeScript → React → Node.js → Databases
Mobile Developer Path: Swift (iOS) or Kotlin (Android) or Dart (Flutter)
Systems Programmer Path: C → C++ → Rust
Data Scientist Path: Python → NumPy/Pandas → Scikit-learn → Deep Learning
DevOps Path: Bash → Python → Go → Terraform
Game Developer Path: C++ or C# (Unity) or GDScript (Godot)
First Language Recommendation: Python for Beginners
Second Language Recommendation: JavaScript for Web
Third Language Recommendation: TypeScript for Scale
3_Language_Features_Comparison: Null Handling: null (Java/JS), nil (Go/Swift/Ruby), None (Python), Option<T> (Rust), T? (Kotlin/Swift/TypeScript)
Error Handling: Exceptions (Java/Python/JS), Result<T,E> (Rust), error return (Go), throws (Swift)
Generics: Java, C++, Rust, TypeScript, Go (1.18+), Swift, Kotlin
Pattern Matching: Rust, Swift, Kotlin, Scala, Python 3.10+, Java 21+
Type Inference: TypeScript, Kotlin, Swift, Rust, Go, C++ (auto)
Operator Overloading: C++, Rust, Kotlin, Swift, Python
Extension Methods/Functions: Kotlin, Swift, C#, TypeScript (via prototype)
Destructuring: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Rust, Kotlin, Swift
String Interpolation: JavaScript, Python, Kotlin, Swift, Ruby, Rust
4_Polyglot_Programming: FFI: Calling C from Python (ctypes, cffi), Rust (bindgen), Go (cgo)
WebAssembly: Running Rust, C++, Go in the Browser
Embedding Languages: Lua in C++, Python in C
Code Style and Conventions Across Languages
Switching Between Languages: Mental Model Shifts
5_Emerging_Languages: Zig: C Alternative with Comptime and No Hidden Control Flow
Nim: Python-like Syntax, Compiles to C
V: Simple, Fast, Safe Systems Language
Mojo: Python Superset for AI/ML Performance
Gleam: Type-safe Language on Erlang VM
Elixir: Functional, Concurrent, on Erlang VM
